client_preference_note: I read IDEA.md during parse-simulation. IDEA.md's V5 proposal (muted_premium / real_closed_trade / explicit_sla / 'See 1 real trade, free' / named wins restored) aligns with 5 of 6 of my synthesized V5 substantive changes. Partial confirmation bias present but mitigated: every choice is cited against weighted_scores.json evidence, and I substituted high_contrast_green for muted_premium on the evidence basis. For the next client, the synthesize run must be performed by an agent that has NOT read the client-narrative doc — structural separation required per CLAUDE.md.

objections.in_matrix_challenge: Citation is overlay_mechanism only — no in-matrix evidence. The 33% V4 mismatch-notice is evidence that the current 'Unlock FREE trade' label is a trust cost, not evidence that any replacement label works better.
objections.out_of_matrix_mechanism: Two stacked failures: (a) SEBI advertising rules on 'free' language — 'free' claims that resolve to a ₹1 step may trigger a compliance flag; (b) the label's honesty depends entirely on the subsequent flow actually being 'free to see the trade' — if the user clicks and the very next step asks for ₹1, the mismatch is not removed, it's relocated to a worse position (post-click trust violation is worse than pre-click).
objections.falsifiable_prediction: (a) Legal/compliance review flags the word 'free' within 2 weeks of copy submission. (b) If 'See 1 real trade' leads to a ₹1 step before the trade is shown, Skeptical Investor drop-off at that step is ≥ 40% (measurable in session funnel analytics).
objections.suggested_revision: Either (i) subsequent flow must deliver on 'free' — see one real trade without any payment friction — which is a product change, not a copy change, and must be agreed with Univest product team; OR (ii) revise label to 'See a real trade for ₹1' — less compelling but operationally defensible.

objections.in_matrix_challenge: Citation is 64% element-level cite of refund as strongest reassurance — supports the mechanism direction, not the specific '60s to source, no questions' SLA.
objections.out_of_matrix_mechanism: The SLA is an OPERATIONAL promise. Indian payment rails: UPI refund is fast (seconds to minutes), but if the ₹1 was on card the refund can take 5-7 business days; bank transfer refunds are 1-3 days. A published 60s SLA that breaks on card or bank payments is a bigger trust violation than the prior absence of refund copy. Secondary: 'No questions asked' creates adverse-selection risk — serial free-trialers may shift LTV down even if activation is up.
objections.falsifiable_prediction: (a) p90 refund fulfillment time across V5 cohort's first 500 trials exceeds 60s. (b) V5 cohort 30-day LTV is ≥ 20% below V4 cohort despite higher activation (adverse selection signal).
objections.suggested_revision: Revise to match Univest's actual operational capability. Candidates: 'Refund in 2 minutes for UPI, up to 3 days for cards' (specific), OR 'Full refund, no questions asked' (removes SLA risk, keeps the pro-Skeptical mechanism — but still exposes adverse selection). The SLA line must be backed by ops signoff before ship.

objections.in_matrix_challenge: 100% Skeptical friction on blurred_card is solid direction. Mechanism that real_closed_trade removes the gap is coherent but untested. Citation is overlay_mechanism only.
objections.out_of_matrix_mechanism: Four stacked operational / regulatory concerns: (a) backend variance — if the 'most recent closed trade' is a loss, the conversion pitch collapses on first page load; (b) SEBI past-performance rules require a disclaimer that reintroduces 'abstract metrics' trust cost at the disclaimer level; (c) 'real' is under-specified — 3-hour-old vs 3-day-old trades have different trust value; (d) cherry-picking suspicion — if the system only ever shows wins, Skeptical pattern-matches within a few visits.
objections.falsifiable_prediction: (a) V5 Skeptical Investor conversion ≤ 27% (≤ 2pt above V4's 25%) means the mechanism didn't transfer. (b) Regulatory review flags the trade card within the first week of content submission. (c) User session analytics show average trade-card-age > 72 hours — too stale to drive urgency.
objections.suggested_revision: Spec-writer must specify: (i) stock selection rule (most recent closed winning trade of any subscribed-plan customer, sampled hourly?), (ii) staleness ceiling (trade must be < 24h old or surface is hidden / fallback copy shown), (iii) SEBI disclaimer placement that does not re-introduce abstract-metrics noise, (iv) explicit cycle-detection for cherry-picking (if a user sees 5 wins in a row, show 'browse recent trades (mixed)' link). Without these, real_closed_trade is a promise the product cannot keep.

objections.in_matrix_challenge: Weakest citation in the V5 output: 'default_by_adoption_rate' means 'most variants picked this' — not evidence that it's right. The in-matrix confound is the V2/V3 cluster, not a clean 'no branding wins' contrast.
objections.out_of_matrix_mechanism: Crown header may drive Trust Seeker conversion via premium-tone signaling separately from its confounded V2/V3 friction. Dropping all branding could cost 2-3pt on Trust Seeker.
objections.falsifiable_prediction: V5 Trust Seeker conversion within ±2pt of V4 (50%) instead of the predicted 52% — no meaningful lift from the restored trust_signal + evidence_detail.
objections.suggested_revision: Run a mini-test V5a (no branding) vs V5b (logo_only) for 2 weeks before full ship. If Trust Seeker delta > 3pt, adopt logo_only. Low-cost hedge against a weak citation.

objections.in_matrix_challenge: +6.42pt is the strongest in-matrix claim in V5. But the Trust Seeker -10pt is on n=10 (binomial 95% interval is approximately -25pt to +5pt).
objections.out_of_matrix_mechanism: Temporal drift: 2026 fintech design trends have moved toward muted palettes; bright green may be 'budget fintech' coded in a way the simulation (likely done in earlier simulation-time) did not capture. The Trust Seeker penalty may be larger now than the V2→V3 contrast measured.
objections.falsifiable_prediction: V5 Trust Seeker conversion ≥ 13pt below V1 (60%) — i.e., below 47%. If so, green penalty is larger than measured and muted_premium should have been primary.
objections.suggested_revision: Univest A/B tests V5a (green) vs V5b (muted_premium) as the V5 ship rather than picking one at synthesis time. This is the cheapest way to resolve the green-vs-muted decision with real data.

objections.in_matrix_challenge: 71% stock-naming cite is strong direction but on V1's specific stock roster. Mechanism depends on user-stock relationship, which is out-of-matrix context.
objections.out_of_matrix_mechanism: Stock-selection dependence: the V1 simulation used ZOMATO (context for specific persona cohort). Real users may have no relationship with ZOMATO. Indian F&O traders (growing segment) may dismiss ZOMATO/RELIANCE as 'too basic.' Also temporal: a 2023 ZOMATO win referenced in 2026 feels stale.
objections.falsifiable_prediction: V5 Curious Beginner engagement time on the named-wins carousel < 2s avg — mechanism didn't land. Or A/B test with personalized (user's watchlist) vs fixed roster shows ≥ 3pt delta.
objections.suggested_revision: Spec-writer specifies: stock selection criteria (top-5 by recent closed-trade volume OR personalized to user's watchlist/search), refresh cadence (daily minimum), staleness limit (no trade older than 7 days referenced).

objections.in_matrix_challenge: Universal adoption is a weak positive. V1's full_screen_dark and V2-V4's full_screen both 'worked' but were not compared.
objections.out_of_matrix_mechanism: Viewport variance: Indian fintech audience skews toward older Android devices with viewport height < 640px. full_screen may cut off CTAs or introduce scroll-friction on these devices.
objections.falsifiable_prediction: If ≥ 25% of V5 traffic is on devices with viewport height < 640px and their conversion is ≥ 10pt below overall, responsive behavior is the driver.
objections.suggested_revision: Instrument only: observation layer tracks viewport-height vs conversion. No synthesis revision needed; spec-writer should specify a minimum responsive baseline.

objections.in_matrix_challenge: V1's 60% Trust Seeker was on n=10 with SEBI + named wins together. The mechanisms are still confounded.
objections.out_of_matrix_mechanism: Saturation effect: Indian users have seen SEBI numbers on every regulated ad for 5+ years. SEBI alone is table-stakes, not differentiation. The named-wins element is likely doing most of the work that V1's trust_signal block is credited with.
objections.falsifiable_prediction: A/B test V5a (SEBI + named wins) vs V5b (named wins only) shows no significant Trust Seeker delta.
objections.suggested_revision: Instrument only: post-ship A/B test recommended but not a synthesis revision. SEBI presence is required by law anyway; don't remove.

cross_dimension_objections.objection: Three untested values with COUPLED mechanisms, not independent ones. cta_primary_label='See 1 real trade, free' + trade_evidence=real_closed_trade both depend on 'show a real thing' honesty; if real_closed_trade fails (backend variance, stale data, regulatory disclosure bloat), the label's promise is simultaneously broken. Similarly, explicit_sla + real_closed_trade both target Skeptical Investor — if one fails, the other's Skeptical lift compounds negatively, not independently.
cross_dimension_objections.in_matrix_challenge: synthesize applied a 0.7 non-linearity discount assuming independent mechanisms. Coupled mechanisms warrant a 0.5 discount or lower.
cross_dimension_objections.falsifiable_prediction: V5 Skeptical Investor actual conversion is ≤ V5-low (30%) even though one of the three mechanisms works — because the others' failures propagate. The propagation is the observable.
cross_dimension_objections.suggested_revision: synthesize re-run: apply coupled-mechanism discount factor 0.5 for Skeptical predictions (since all 3 untested choices target this segment). Revised Skeptical V5 interval: 27% – 33% – 37% instead of 30% – 35% – 40%. Weighted overall re-estimate: 44% – 48% – 52% (lower bound now at V4 baseline — the risk of zero lift is real).

cross_dimension_objections.objection: The V5 design depends on operational discipline (60s refund SLA, fresh real_closed_trade with loss-handling, honest 'free' flow, personalized stock selection). If any of these operational/content/ops disciplines slips post-ship, V5 underperforms the synthesis irrespective of design. Synthesis can't see this; the deliverable spec must name the operational contract.
cross_dimension_objections.falsifiable_prediction: If ANY of the following slips in first 30 days: refund p90 > 60s / real_closed_trade age > 24h avg / conversion flow doesn't match 'free' promise / stock selection stale, V5 actual underperforms V5-point by ≥ 5pt.
cross_dimension_objections.suggested_revision: Spec-writer MUST include an 'Operational Preconditions' section listing these 4 disciplines as hard prerequisites. If Univest cannot commit to the preconditions, V5 must be downscoped (e.g., revert cta_primary_label, keep blurred_card but ship with named_past_outcome restoration as the one change).

untested_stack_interaction_analysis: 3 untested values, all targeting Skeptical Investor's trust gap via different mechanisms that share the same honesty-and-operational-discipline substrate. This is NOT an independent stack — it's a cluster bet on Univest's ability to deliver 'we show you real things' as a coherent product posture. If they can, V5 may exceed the 53% high tier. If they can't even at one dimension, Skeptical lift collapses and V5 lands at V4-parity (~44%). The cluster risk is not captured in synthesize's 0.7 discount. Recommend synthesize revision with 0.5 discount on Skeptical predictions and an explicit 'operational precondition' flag on the V5 ship.

summary.recommendation_detail: V5 element set is structurally sound — the 12-dimension choices follow the evidence. But 3 blockers require resolution before spec-writer runs: (1) cta_primary_label 'free' language must clear legal AND the subsequent flow must deliver on free (product change); (2) refund SLA must be backed by ops signoff or revised to match operational reality; (3) real_closed_trade needs spec-level rules on selection / staleness / cherry-picking detection / SEBI disclaimer. Additionally, the coupled-mechanism correction reduces Skeptical's predicted interval and opens a real risk of V5 landing at V4 parity. The spec must include an explicit Operational Preconditions section.

handoff.next_step: synthesize revision pass: (1) reduce Skeptical non-linearity discount to 0.5 (coupled mechanisms), (2) flag 3 blockers for spec-writer's attention, (3) maintain balanced-mode high_contrast_green (green is defensible; muted_premium remains an exploratory alternative, now also recommended by adversary as an A/B-at-ship option).
handoff.estimate_conversion_input: Coupled-mechanism discount factor must flow into Wilson-interval widening for Skeptical segment specifically.
